Title: Overexpression of a developing xylem cDNA library in transgenic poplar generates high mutation rate specific to wood formation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural secondary cell wall component important for wood formation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: IRX9 glycosyltransferase-related non-CLS cell wall protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Recovered genes were predominantly highest in xylem; this gene was discussed as a cell wall biosynthesis-related homolog
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in FOX line 1F3-9 under CaMV 35S
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with altered lignin/moisture-related wood traits in a FOX line and discussed as involved in xylan biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FOX overexpression screen; insert recovery and sequencing; expression analysis of inserted FLcDNA; trait profiling
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Published RNA-seq expression dataset from Shi et al. 2017; accession not provided
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for modifying secondary wall composition in wood
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned by authors as a homolog of a known Arabidopsis gene involved in cell wall biosynthesis; direct causal recapitulation not shown in this paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A FOX mutant line carried an IRX9-homolog FLcDNA. 2. IRX9 is cited as a non-CLS gene involved in xylan biosynthesis. 3. Its recovery supports enrichment of wood cell-wall related genes in the xylem-derived FOX library.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major secondary cell wall components contributing to wood properties
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Recovered genes were predominantly expressed in xylem
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in FOX line 3F90-4 under CaMV 35S
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with altered cellulose-related cell wall composition in a FOX mutant; authors cite FLA knockdown literature linking FLA17-like genes to reduced cellulose and lignin
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FOX overexpression screen; insert sequencing; trait profiling; qRT-PCR of FLcDNA overexpression
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Published RNA-seq expression dataset from Shi et al. 2017; accession not provided
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for engineering wood cell wall composition
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive rather than definitive within this paper; mechanistic validation not performed here
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLA17-homolog FLcDNA was recovered from a cell wall-related FOX mutant. 2. Authors interpret this as consistent with known roles of FLA genes in cellulose/lignin deposition. 3. The result supports tissue-biased discovery of wood formation genes.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural secondary cell wall polymer in wood
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Recovered FLcDNA homologs were often highest in xylem
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in FOX line 1F2-4 under CaMV 35S
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with altered cellulose/hemicellulose and lignin traits; discussed as organizing microtubule scaffolds associated with cellulose assembly
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FOX overexpression screen; FLcDNA sequencing; qRT-PCR; wood chemistry trait profiling
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Published RNA-seq expression dataset from Shi et al. 2017; accession not provided
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for modifying cellulose deposition through cell wall assembly processes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a catalytic pathway enzyme or TF; included as a common gene because it affects wall formation through microtubule organization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A MAP65-8 homolog was recovered in a mutant with altered wall composition traits. 2. Authors note MAP65 proteins organize microtubule scaffolds linked to cellulose assembly. 3. Recovery of this gene further supports enrichment for xylem/secondary wall biology in the FOX population.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes xylem proliferation and secondary growth
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly converts inactive cytokinin precursor iPRMP to the biologically active cytokinin iP in a single step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key activation step enabling tissue-specific control of bioactive cytokinin levels; rate-limiting not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest native expression in xylem; majority of poplar LOG genes are expressed in wood-forming tissues with zone-specific profiles
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed under CaMV 35S in transgenic poplar; native homolog expression highest in xylem/wood-forming tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases bioactive cytokinin (iP) levels and strongly promotes xylem proliferation, increasing stem diameter while decreasing height
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FOX overexpression mutant identification; qRT-PCR; cytokinin LC-MS/MS profiling; retransformation/overexpression recapitulation in multiple independent lines; anatomical measurement of xylem width
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published RNA-seq expression datasets from Shi et al. 2017 and AspWood/Sundell et al. 2017; accession numbers not provided in text
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ubiquitous overexpression simultaneously increased girth and decreased height growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase woody biomass via enhanced stem girth; preferable deployment with xylem-specific promoter to avoid pleiotropic height reduction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The inserted FLcDNA in line 3F16-6 was homologous to Arabidopsis LOG1 and P. trichocarpa LOG1; authors collectively refer to both as poplar LOG1. Increased precursor depletion was significant in leaves but not stems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 3F16-6 contained a single overexpressed FLcDNA encoding poplar LOG1. 2. LOG1 overexpression increased active cytokinin iP in stems and leaves, consistent with direct cytokinin activation from iPRMP. 3. Multiple independent overexpression lines phenocopied the original mutant, confirming LOG1 causally promotes xylem proliferation and stem diameter.
